_meta.description: Year-over-year regulation changes that affect strategy logic. Only changes that materially impact your MDP are listed — cosmetic rewordings and administrative changes are excluded. Each entry describes what changed, why it matters to your system, and which file/field it overrides or extends.
_meta.how_to_use: Load this alongside f1_tyre_constraints.json and f1_soft_rules.json. When simulating a historical race, apply the diffs for that year on top of the base soft rules. Your system should index by year before each simulation.

2018._summary: Baseline year. No significant strategy-relevant changes from 2017 that affect your 2018–2026 scope.

q2_tyre_rule.description: Top 10 qualifiers must start the race on the tyre used to set their fastest Q2 lap time.
q2_tyre_rule.strategy_impact: Teams in Q3 often deliberately use medium or hard in Q2 to avoid starting on softs. Do not assume all top-10 cars start on the softest compound.
q2_tyre_rule.mdp_action: Check Q2 tyre compound per driver before assigning race start compound. Soft assumption is not valid for Q3 runners.

tyre_compound_range.note: 7 compound range in 2018 (Hypersoft to Superhard). Pirelli selects 3 per race. Does not affect set counts.

2019._summary: Teams could choose their own compound breakdown within the 13-set allocation. Strategic impact on compound distribution.

compound_selection.description: Unlike 2020+, teams in 2019 chose how many sets of each compound they received. E.g. one team might take 1 hard / 4 medium / 8 soft while another takes 0 hard / 3 medium / 10 soft.
compound_selection.strategy_impact: You cannot assume a fixed 2H/3M/8S breakdown for 2019. Compound availability varies per team per race.
compound_selection.mdp_action: For 2019 historical simulations, load the actual per-team compound selection from FastF1 data rather than assuming the fixed allocation.

2020._summary: Fixed tyre allocation introduced due to COVID-19 pandemic. Teams no longer choose compound breakdown.

q2_tyre_rule.description: Proposal to drop Q2 rule for 2020 failed to get unanimous team agreement — rule stayed.
q2_tyre_rule.note: At least one team blocked the change. Do not incorrectly set this to false for 2020.

compound_selection.description: Pirelli sets the allocation for all teams — same breakdown for every driver. Teams no longer choose.
compound_selection.strategy_impact: From 2020 onwards you can safely assume the published fixed allocation (2H/3M/8S for standard GPs). Eliminates team compound-choice as a strategic variable.
compound_selection.mdp_action: Use fixed compound breakdown from f1_tyre_constraints.json for 2020+. No need to query per-team selection.

2021._summary: Sprint qualifying introduced mid-season. Q2 rule still active. Parc fermé trigger differs on sprint weekends.

q2_tyre_rule.description: Still active for 2021. Dropped at end of season for 2022 onwards.
q2_tyre_rule.sprint_weekend_exception: Q2 rule does NOT apply on sprint weekends in 2021 — drivers have free tyre choice for the race start.
q2_tyre_rule.mdp_action: On sprint weekends in 2021: no Q2 tyre constraint on race start compound. On standard weekends: Q2 rule applies.

sprint_format.description: Sprint qualifying sets the grid for the sprint race. Sprint result sets the grid for the GP. Parc fermé begins after sprint qualifying ends.
sprint_format.strategy_impact: On sprint weekends: 12 dry sets instead of 13. Tyre strategy for both sprint and GP must fit within the reduced allocation.
sprint_format.mdp_action: Detect sprint weekend flag. Switch to sprint allocation from f1_tyre_constraints.json. Parc fermé applies from end of sprint qualifying, not Q1.

quali_engine_modes.description: FIA banned the use of special high-power qualifying engine modes from 2021. All sessions must use the same power unit mode.
quali_engine_modes.strategy_impact: Qualifying pace gap between teams narrowed slightly. Does not directly affect race strategy logic but affects pace benchmarks.
quali_engine_modes.mdp_action: No direct encoding needed — affects pace model inputs, not constraint logic.

2022._summary: Major regulation reset. New technical regs (18-inch wheels, ground effect aero). Q2 rule dropped. Parc fermé sprint trigger continues.

q2_tyre_rule.description: Scrapped from 2022 onwards. All drivers have free tyre choice for race start regardless of qualifying position.
q2_tyre_rule.strategy_impact: Top-10 starters no longer locked into Q2 compound. Expect more strategic variety on race starts — softs not guaranteed for P1-10.
q2_tyre_rule.mdp_action: Remove Q2 compound constraint from race start state. All positions have free compound choice.

technical_regulations.strategy_impact: New tyre construction changes degradation behaviour significantly vs 2021. Do not use 2021 degradation models for 2022 races.
technical_regulations.mdp_action: Treat 2022 as a new degradation baseline. Historical training data from 2021 should not be used for 2022+ degradation regression.

2023._summary: Sprint format restructured — Sprint Shootout replaces Sprint Qualifying. Functionally similar but renamed sessions.

compound_rules_in_shootout.note: Unlike 2024+, no mandatory compound rules per shootout segment in 2023.

sprint_format.strategy_impact: 6 sprint weekends in 2023 vs 3 in 2021/2022 — more races where you need sprint allocation logic.
sprint_format.mdp_action: Parc fermé trigger renamed but functionally identical. Update session name reference if using API session labels.

alternative_tyre_allocation.description: FIA trialled a reduced 11-set allocation (instead of 13) at 2 races in 2023 with different mandatory compound rules per qualifying segment.
alternative_tyre_allocation.strategy_impact: At these 2 races, standard allocation assumptions are wrong. Qualifying tyre strategy is also constrained per segment.
alternative_tyre_allocation.mdp_action: Flag the 2 ATA races in your race metadata. Apply 11-set allocation and mandatory qualifying compound rules for those events only.

2024._summary: Wet tyre allocation restructured (5 inter / 2 wet vs 4/3 previously). Sprint shootout gets mandatory compounds per segment.

tyre_allocation_change.description: Intermediate sets increased from 4 to 5. Full wet sets decreased from 3 to 2. Confirmed mid-2024 after Japan controversy.
tyre_allocation_change.reason: Teams no longer need to return an intermediate set after Friday use — the extra set compensates. Side effect: teams conserved inters on Friday, causing the Suzuka FP2 controversy.
tyre_allocation_change.mdp_action: Load 2024+ tyre allocation from f1_tyre_constraints.json. Do not use 2023 inter/wet counts for 2024+ simulations.

sprint_shootout_compounds.description: From 2024, mandatory new compounds per sprint shootout segment introduced.
sprint_shootout_compounds.strategy_impact: Compound usage in sprint shootout is now regulated — affects how many sets of each compound remain for sprint race and GP.
sprint_shootout_compounds.mdp_action: Deduct one new medium set (SQ1+SQ2) and one new soft set (SQ3) from sprint weekend allocation when simulating 2024+ sprint weekends.

2025._summary: Monaco mandatory two-stop rule introduced. Tyre allocation same as 2024. Sprint format continues.

monaco_two_stop_rule.description: Each driver must use at least three sets of tyres (two different compounds if dry) during the Monaco GP. Introduced after the 2024 Monaco race where a red flag allowed a zero-stop race.
monaco_two_stop_rule.strategy_impact: Monaco is no longer a potential one-stop or zero-stop race. Minimum two pit stops required. Significantly changes Monaco strategy.
monaco_two_stop_rule.mdp_action: For Monaco 2025: set minimum_pit_stops = 2. Mandatory tyre sets = 3. Apply before evaluating any Monaco strategy.

2026._summary: Full regulation reset. New power unit (more electrical, less fuel), narrower cars, active aero. Tyre allocation returns to 4 inter / 3 wet. Monaco two-stop rule dropped. Q3 duration extended to 13 minutes.

technical_regulations.strategy_impact: New car concept means all pre-2026 degradation and pace models are invalid for 2026. Treat as a new baseline.
technical_regulations.mdp_action: Do not use 2022–2025 degradation regression outputs for 2026 simulations. Require 2026-specific training data.

tyre_allocation_change.description: Returns to 4 intermediate sets and 3 full wet sets — same as pre-2024. Confirmed by Mercedes Australia race notes.
tyre_allocation_change.mdp_action: Load 2026 tyre allocation from f1_tyre_constraints.json. Do not carry over 2024/2025 inter/wet counts.

monaco_two_stop_rule.description: FIA dropped the Monaco mandatory two-stop rule for 2026. Standard two-compound rule applies.
monaco_two_stop_rule.mdp_action: Monaco 2026 uses standard mandatory_dry_compounds: 2 rule. No minimum pit stop count.

qualifying_format.strategy_impact: Slightly longer Q3 — marginal effect on tyre usage. Grid of 22 means more cars in Q1/Q2 elimination.
qualifying_format.mdp_action: No direct race strategy impact. Update grid size to 22 for 2026 simulations.
